p.em: Proceed in your native language if you wish. Instructions will remain in English.

ut:template.p: If all actions produce the expected results described, please submit a 'passed' result.
If any action fails, or produces an unexpected result, please submit a 'failed' result and file a bug. Please be sure to include the bug number when you submit your result.

ut:test.dt: Boot up the image
ut:test.dd: If you see the GRUB boot menu you should see the following:
The system boots properly and loads the installer displaying the Welcome dialog with language selection and 'Try FAMILY' and 'Install FAMILY' buttons
ut:test.dt: Click on the release notes hyperlink to confirm that a browser launches and you are taken to the release notes discourse page.
Click on the Install FAMILY button
ut:test.dd: The 'Keyboard layout' screen appears
The proposed keyboard corresponds with your keyboard
ut:test.dt: Select your keyboard layout and click on Continue
ut:test.dd: The 'Updates and other software' screen is displayed
ut:test.dt: On the screen 'Updates and other software', note the availability of the following components
ut:test.dd: Available options should represent the state of your system accurately

For Mantic and later releases, the default install is a minimal install. Releases before Mantic the default is a 'full' install, complete with other apps a user might want. The new, default, install does not include these add-ons.

ul.li: (If network is available) 'Download updates while installing FAMILY'
(If on a 'laptop') 'Is plugged to a power source'

ut:test.dt: Click on the Continue button
ut:test.dd: The 'Installation type' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on the Continue button (if there is only one hard disk in the system, the button should read 'Install Now')
ut:test.dd: 'Write the changes to disks' dialogue appears
ut:test.dt: Click Continue
ut:test.dd: If there is only one hard disk, the installer skips to the "Where are you?' screen. Otherwise, the 'Installation type' screen is displayed
ut:test.dt: If there is only one hard disk, skip to step 12 (On the 'Where are you?' screen...). Otherwise, on the 'Installation type' page, select a drive in the 'Select drive' list.
ut:test.dd: Verify that the selected drive corresponds to the drive on the chart
ut:test.dt: Verify that the full drive space is allocated
ut:test.dd: Full drive space is allocated for installation
ut:test.dt: Click on the Install Now button
ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Advanced Features' dialog is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select 'Use LVM with the new FAMILY installation' and check 'Encrypt the new FAMILY installation for security'
ut:test.dd: 'Encrypt the new FAMILY Installation for security' and 'Use LVM with the new FAMILY Installation' is checked
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'OK' button
ut:test.dd: The dialog closes and 'LVM and encryption selected' is displayed next to the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Install Now' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Choose a security key' screen is displayed with the 'Install Now' button greyed out
ut:test.dt: Enter a security key and type a different key into the 'confirm the security key' input box
ut:test.dd: The installer displays 'Passwords do not match'
ut:test.dt: Enter a security key and type the same key into the 'Confirm the security key' input box
ut:test.dd: The passwords are accepted and the 'Install Now' button can be clicked
ut:test.dt: Check 'Overwrite empty disk space'
Click on the Continue button (if there is only one hard disk in the system, the button should read 'Install Now')
ut:test.dd: 'Write the changes to disks' dialogue appears
ut:test.dt: Click Continue

If there is only one hard disk, skip to step 18 (On the 'Where are you?' screen...). Otherwise, on the 'Installation type' screen verify that the drive selected on the Select drive list corresponds to the drive on the chart (e.g /dev/sda)
ut:test.dd: Selected drive is displayed on the chart
ut:test.dt: Verify that the full drive space is allocated
ut:test.dd: Full drive space is allocated for installation
ut:test.dt: Click on the Install Now button
ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Advanced Features' dialog is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select 'Erase disk and use ZFS'
ut:test.dd: 'Erase disk and use ZFS' is selected
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'OK' button
ut:test.dd: The dialog closes and 'ZFS selected' is displayed next to the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Install Now' button
ut:test.dd: 'Write the changes to disks' dialogue appears
ut:test.dt: Click Continue

If there is only one hard disk, skip to step 18 (On the 'Where are you?' screen...). Otherwise, on the 'Installation type' screen verify that the drive selected on the Select drive list corresponds to the drive on the chart (e.g /dev/sda)
ut:test.dd: Selected drive is displayed on the chart
ut:test.dt: Verify that the full drive space is allocated
ut:test.dd: Full drive space is allocated for installation
ut:test.dt: Click on the Install Now button
ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: Note the state of the 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button
ut:test.dd: The 'Erase disk and install FAMILY' radio button is selected and the 'Advanced features' button is active
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dd: The 'Advanced Features' dialog is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select 'Erase disk and use ZFS'
ut:test.dd: 'Erase disk and use ZFS' is selected
Check the tickbox for 'Encrypt the new Ubuntu installation for security'
ut:test.dt: Click on the 'OK' button
ut:test.dd: The dialog closes and 'ZFS selected' is displayed next to the 'Advanced features...' button
ut:test.dt: Click Continue
The "Choose a security key" slide opens
ut:test.dd: Enter your desired security key
ut:test.dt: Tick the "Enable recovery key" checkbox
ut:test.dd: Enter your desired recovery key
Click the downwards arrow icon, and click "Open" in the popup window
ut:test.dt: Open a terminal window and run the following commands:
ut:test.dd: cat /home/ubuntu/recovery.key
ut:test.dt: Verify the recovery key matches your inputted recovery key
Close the terminal window
Click on the 'Install Now' button
ut:test.dd: 'Write the changes to disks' dialogue appears
ut:test.dt: Click Continue

If there is only one hard disk, skip to step 18 (On the 'Where are you?' screen...). Otherwise, on the 'Installation type' screen verify that the drive selected on the Select drive list corresponds to the drive on the chart (e.g /dev/sda)
ut:test.dd: Selected drive is displayed on the chart
ut:test.dt: Verify that the full drive space is allocated
ut:test.dd: Full drive space is allocated for installation
ut:test.dt: Click on the Install Now button
ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: Select Install FAMILY XX.XX alongside SYSTEM YY
ut:test.dd: (SYSTEM YY is the name of the system already installed on disk (FAMILY 12.04, Windows 7, ...)
ut:test.dt: Click on Continue
ut:test.dd: The Screen Install FAMILY XX.XX alongside SYSTEM YY appears
ut:test.dt: Note the drive selected on the Select drive list and the bar state
ut:test.strong: If the target drive has a free partition with sufficient freespace, install will proceed without further partitioning intervention
ut:test.dd: The drive corresponds to the drive on the chart (e.g /dev/sda) and the bar is divided
ut:test.dt: Move the greyslider bar that separates the blue sections as appropriate
ut:test.dd: The slider bar can be set as appropriate
ut:test.dt: Click on the Install Now button
A dialog will appear asking if you want to write the changes to disk. Click Continue.
ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: Select Something Else
ut:test.dd: A screen showing the current hard disks and partition layouts is displayed
ut:test.dt: Select the drive you wish to partition and use the Add '+', Change 'Change...', and Delete '-' buttons to create your desired scheme
ut:test.dd: The screen updates showing your desired partitions and mount points
ut:test.dt: Once you have your required partitioning scheme laid out, click on the Install Now button
ut:test.dd: The 'Where are you?' screen is displayed

ut:test.dt: If your system is connected to the network, note the preselected timezone corresponds with your timezone and the city indicated in the text box
ut:test.dd: The timezone and city displayed match your timezone and a major city from your area
ut:test.dt: Select your timezone, and click on the Continue button
ut:test.dd: The 'Who are you?' screen appears
ut:test.dt: Input your initial user details and password admin can not be used - it is a dedicated Linux User
ut:test.dd: 'Require my password to log in' is shown and selected or if 'Log in automatically' and 'require my password to log in' are shown then 'Require my password to login' is selected. If just 'Require my password to log in' is shown, having it off is the equivalent of having 'Log in automatically' on.
Name, username and password are accepted.
Continue button becomes available
ut:test.dt: Press Continue
ut:test.dd: The 'Welcome to FAMILY' slide is displayed
The slideshow is entirely in your language
ut:test.dt: Wait for the installer to finish
ut:test.dd: An 'Installation Complete' dialog appears
ut:test.dt: Click the 'Restart Now' button
ut:test.dd: GUI is shut down, a prompt to remove media and press Enter appears
ut:test.dt: Remove the disc and press enter
ut:test.dd: The machine is rebooted

ut:test.dt: Allow the machine to reboot
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly and loads into FAMILY showing username selected

ut:test.dt: Allow the machine to reboot
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly
A field is displayed to enter your password to decrypt the disk
The system loads into FAMILY showing username selected

ut:test.dt: Allow the machine to reboot
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly
The system loads into FAMILY showing username selected
Upon login, open a terminal, run the following commands and verify it matches the output

dd.code: $ zfs mount | sort bpool/BOOT/ubuntu_UUID /boot rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID / rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/srv /srv rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/usr/local /usr/local rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/games /var/games rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/AccountsService /var/lib/AccountsService rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/apt /var/lib/apt rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/dpkg /var/lib/dpkg rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/NetworkManager /var/lib/NetworkManager rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib /var/lib rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/log /var/log rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/mail /var/mail rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/snap /var/snap rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/spool /var/spool rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/www /var/www rpool/USERDATA/root_0y7dio /root rpool/USERDATA/u_0y7dio /home/u

ut:test.dt: Allow the machine to reboot
ut:test.dd: The system boots properly and your passphrase works
The system loads into FAMILY showing username selected
Upon login, open a terminal, run the following commands and verify it matches the output

dd.code: $ zfs mount | sort bpool/BOOT/ubuntu_UUID /boot rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID / rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/srv /srv rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/usr/local /usr/local rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/games /var/games rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/AccountsService /var/lib/AccountsService rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/apt /var/lib/apt rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/dpkg /var/lib/dpkg rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib/NetworkManager /var/lib/NetworkManager rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/lib /var/lib rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/log /var/log rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/mail /var/mail rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/snap /var/snap rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/spool /var/spool rpool/ROOT/ubuntu_UUID/var/www /var/www rpool/USERDATA/root_0y7dio /root rpool/USERDATA/u_0y7dio /home/u

ut:test.dt: Run the lsblk command and check for this line in the output:
ut:test.dd: cryptoswap 252:1 0 2.6G 0 crypt [SWAP]

ut:test.dt: Allow the machine to reboot, select the first option at the GRUB menu
ut:test.dd: Your new installation boots
ut:test.dt: Reboot machine, select the previously installed system
ut:test.dd: Previously installed system boots and operates as expected

ut:test.dt: Start the session
ut:test.dd: The initial setup should autostart and the 'Online Accounts' screen display and the following account types should be displayed
ut:test.dt: Select one of the accounts and go through the steps to configure it. If you are not connected to the Internet skip this test.
ut:test.dd: The accounts list should be displayed again but with the configure account ID displayed on the corresponding entry
ut:test.dt: Click 'Next'
ut:test.dd: The 'Help improve Ubuntu' screen is displayed and the 'Yes, send system info to Canonical' option is selected
ut:test.dt: Click 'Show the First Report'
ut:test.dd: A new dialog opens showing the collect information
ut:test.dt: Verify that the report's contents makes sense and that it doesn't include anything private or that shouldn't be collected
Close the dialog and click 'Legal notice'
ut:test.dd: The default webbrowser opens and display the 'Legal Notice – System Information' page from the Canonical website
ut:test.dt: Close the browser, go back to the wizard and click 'Next'
ut:test.dd: The 'privacy' screen is displayed, the location services toggle is off
ut:test.dt: Go to Settings->Privacy screen and check that the status
ut:test.dd: It should reflect the wizard status
ut:test.dt: Go back to the wizard and change the toggle status
ut:test.dd: The settings screen show reflect the status change
ut:test.dt: Click 'Next'
ut:test.dd: The 'Ready to go' screen is displayed including a grid of icons representing popular software
ut:test.dt: If you are connected to the Internet click on one of the software
ut:test.dd: 'Ubuntu Software' is started and show the page corresponding to the selected software
ut:test.dt: Click 'Install'
ut:test.dd: The software should install and be available once it's finished
ut:test.dt: Close the store, go back to the wizard and click 'Open Ubuntu Software now'
ut:test.dd: 'Ubuntu Software' is started and displays the frontpage
ut:test.dt: Close the store, go back to the wizard and click 'Done'
ut:test.dd: The wizard closes, and you have a working desktop session
ut:test.dt: In a terminal run '$ ubuntu-report send yes'
ut:test.dd: If you are connected to the Internet it should tell you that the report for that machine has already been sent. If you are not connected it should retry later or on next login.

ut:case.dt: Go through an installation picking your preferred partitioning method, in the 'Updates and other software' select 'Minimal installation'
ut:case.dd: On the installed system check that none of the libreoffice, rhythmbox, totem, thunderbird, cheese, shotwell software are installed
